name: Mobility Partners Of Houston
recent_news: BBB-listed mobility equipment dealer selling wheelchair vans, accessible SUVs/trucks, scooters, lifts and home accessibility products in the Houston area; not a school-bus or student-transport contractor.
fit_notes: Not a fit. Mobility equipment retailer (wheelchair-accessible vehicle sales/conversions), not a student-transportation operator. Exclude from tuck-in pipeline.

name: PCC Transportation LLC
recent_news: Small Moseley/Powhatan-area accessible transportation provider listed on Pegbo, My Mobility Path and Virginia accessible-transport directories; no public school-district contracts identified.
fit_notes: Marginal. Sole-prop/very small NEMT-style accessible operator (~5 employees, ~$316K rev); not a yellow-bus school contractor. Sub-scale for a Summit/I Squared tuck-in.

name: Carey/Phoenix Limousine
districts_or_customers: Arizona corporate, prom, group charter clients
recent_news: Arizona-based executive limo/charter operator (sedans, SUVs, vans, 23-40 pax mini-coaches) serving since 1978; despite Iowa registration address, operations are in Phoenix AZ as Carey International affiliate. No school-bus or special-needs school transport found.
fit_notes: Not a fit. Executive limousine / corporate charter operator (Carey network), not a school-bus contractor. Exclude from tuck-in pipeline.

name: Major H Svc LLC
districts_or_customers: VA hospitals and veteran patients in Sacramento/Roseville/Rocklin/Lincoln area
recent_news: Roseville-based non-emergency medical transport provider focused on veterans and disabled patients to/from VA hospitals and medical appointments; registered as NEMT van provider on NPI directories.
fit_notes: Not a fit. NEMT-only veteran/medical transport (~4 employees, ~$311K rev); no school-bus or SPED student-transport offering. Exclude.

name: T LC Transportation
districts_or_customers: Greater Toledo / Northwest Ohio school districts (supplemental K-12)
recent_news: TLC Transit (Holland, OH) grew from a 15-van startup in 2017 to billed as 'Ohio's largest NEMT fleet,' serving 10,000+ annual passengers; member of UT Family Business Center and Sylvania Chamber. Founder Jon Elston previously led Toledo Area Paratransit and Lucas County DD transportation.
fit_notes: Possible adjacent fit. Family-owned NW Ohio NEMT/student-transport hybrid with paratransit DNA and supplemental K-12 contracts — interesting bolt-on for Summit's SPED/alt-transport sleeve in Ohio, though core is NEMT, not yellow-bus. Founder is relatively young (still operating)/no obvious succession trigger; Vanessa Dunton (listed as CEO in source data) not confirmed in public materials — owner of record is Jon Elston.
